growth_records.strain: BM1 (earliest of 22 sequential isolates from a chronically infected cystic fibrosis patient, first isolated 1993)
growth_records.cite: Silva et al. Long-Term Evolution of Burkholderia multivorans during a Chronic Cystic Fibrosis Infection Reveals Shifting Forces of Selection. mSystems (2016). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00029-16. [PMC5069766]
growth_records.snippet: the in vitro doubling times and biomass yields of the isolates were estimated in synthetic cystic fibrosis medium. Compared to the doubling times of 6 h for isolate BM1 and of 6.2 to 6.3 h for clade C4 isolates, all isol
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (6 h) for isolate BM1 reported directly in text (microplate-reader OD640 growth-curve fitting during exponential phase, mean of >=3 independent experiments). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/doubling_time = 0.6931/6 = 0.1155 h-1 (calculated).

growth_records.strain: clade C4 isolates (later CF isolates, phylogenetic clade C4)
growth_records.cite: Silva et al. Long-Term Evolution of Burkholderia multivorans during a Chronic Cystic Fibrosis Infection Reveals Shifting Forces of Selection. mSystems (2016). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00029-16. [PMC5069766]
growth_records.snippet: Compared to the doubling times of 6 h for isolate BM1 and of 6.2 to 6.3 h for clade C4 isolates, all isolates from clades C1 (P<0.01), C2, and C3 (P<0.05) needed between 7 and 15 h to duplicate
growth_records.notes: Doubling-time range (6.2 to 6.3 h) for clade C4 isolates reported directly in text. growth_rate_per_h range = ln2/6.2=0.1118 h-1 to ln2/6.3=0.1100 h-1 (calculated), reported here as the range 0.110-0.112 h-1. Doubling times of clade C4 isolates did NOT differ significantly from BM1 per the paper's statistics.

growth_records.strain: clades C1, C2, C3 isolates (later CF isolates, phylogenetic clades C1-C3)
growth_records.cite: Silva et al. Long-Term Evolution of Burkholderia multivorans during a Chronic Cystic Fibrosis Infection Reveals Shifting Forces of Selection. mSystems (2016). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00029-16. [PMC5069766]
growth_records.snippet: all isolates from clades C1 (P<0.01), C2, and C3 (P<0.05) needed between 7 and 15 h to duplicate (see Fig. S2A)
growth_records.notes: Doubling-time range (7 to 15 h) reported directly in text for pooled isolates of clades C1, C2 and C3 (significantly slower than ancestral BM1). growth_rate_per_h range = ln2/15=0.0462 h-1 to ln2/7=0.0990 h-1 (calculated), reported as range 0.046-0.099 h-1. Confidence medium because this is an aggregated range across three distinct clades/multiple isolates rather than a single strain value.
